name: Lucky Jack The Lord of Thieves
description:
Lucky Jack The Lord of Thieves

*{Lore Lucky Jack was once a mortal --- a thief so skilled that even the gods took notice. He stole a coin from the Hollow's own pocket, and the Hollow laughed. "You are clever," it said. "But cleverness is a debt. One day, I will collect." Jack has been collecting luck ever since.

He is the Unspent Coin, the Magpie King, the patron of urchins, beggars, and thieves who plan their work and work their plan. His followers are not murderers --- they are professionals. They know that the greatest heist is the one that leaves no trace, no victims, and no debts. Yet every stroke of luck is a loan, and Jack's interest rates are legendary. When he comes to collect, you will wish you had never met him.

Among the peoples of Fate's Edge, Lucky Jack is known by many names. In Silkstrand, he is the Cut Purse's Blessing, whispered by pickpockets who know that a moment's distraction is worth more than a year's labor. In Vhasia, he is the Gorget-Breaker, a patron of disgraced knights who have turned to thievery. In Zakov, he is the Tide-That-Eats-The-Shore, a slow, patient consumption that reshapes coastlines. In the Brass Coast, he is the Freedman's Luck, invoked by escaped slaves who steal to survive. Wherever a lock is picked, a purse is cut, or a plan comes together at the last possible second, Lucky Jack is already there --- tipping his cap and counting the interest.

quote "Luck is not a gift. It is a loan. Pay it forward, or pay it dearly. The coin that never spends is the debt that never dies." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize The Heist: planning, execution, and the perfect getaway Luck as Resource: borrowing fortune, repaying obligation, the gamble that pays off The Unpayable Debt: every favor must be repaid; every stroke of luck is a loan with interest The Underworld: gutters, rooftops, and the spaces between laws itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Lucky Jack does not bind a creature of hearth and home. Their Thiasos is a small creature of luck and observation: a magpie that collects shiny objects and knows every hiding place, a mouse that nests in counting-houses and nibbles the corners of unpaid bills, a spider that weaves webs in the shape of a question mark, or a cricket that chirps only when fortune is about to turn. This creature requires no food, but it must witness a successful theft or a clever deception each day. If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (it grows listless and its predictions turn sour, imposing --1 die on all Subterfuge and Stealth rolls for its bearer until they pull off a successful heist).

The Codex of Lucky Jack is never a book of prayers. It is a stolen ledger, a floor plan of a place you have never robbed, or a collection of coins from failed heists. Because luck resists being written down, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week adding a new entry --- a plan, a sketch, a note on a mark's habits --- or the Rites stored there become stale (treat as Compromised until you case a new location).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Lucky Jack may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or a coin) and whisper a word of luck. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Subterfuge or Stealth, and the first time each scene the bearer would roll a 1 on a roll involving deception or theft, they may reroll that die. After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Lucky Jack.
summary: Lucky Jack The Lord of Thieves *{Lore Lucky Jack was once a mortal --- a thief so skilled that even the gods took notice. He stole a coin from the Hollow's own pocket, and the Hollow laughed. "You are clever," it said. "But cleverness is a debt. One day, I will collect.

rites.name: Rite of the Lucky Coin
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Instant; Self; No. Tags: , , Materials: A coin that has been in your pocket for at least a day. Effect: Spend 1 Boon to reroll one die that came up 1. You must keep the new result. If the reroll is also a 1, the GM gains 2 SB instead of 1 --- the luck has turned against you. Push It: Reroll two dice instead of one, but you must keep the second result. Cost: Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: Spend 1 Boon to reroll one die that came up 1. You must keep the new result. If the reroll is also a 1, the GM gains 2 SB instead of 1 --- the luck has turned against you. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Clever Hand
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , , Materials: A piece of string tied in a simple knot. Effect: Gain +2 dice on a single Subterfuge, Craft, or Sleight of Hand roll. If you fail, the GM gains 1 SB as your hand slips at the worst moment. Push It: Extend the bonus to one ally within Near. Cost: Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Gain +2 dice on a single Subterfuge, Craft, or Sleight of Hand roll. If you fail, the GM gains 1 SB as your hand slips at the worst moment. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Quick Exit
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Instant; Self; No. Tags: , , Materials: A handful of dust or sand, thrown over your shoulder. Effect: When you would be caught or cornered, you may reroll one failed escape roll. If you succeed, the GM gains 1 SB (your escape was too close). Push It: Also gain +1 Position on your next action. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: When you would be caught or cornered, you may reroll one failed escape roll. If you succeed, the GM gains 1 SB (your escape was too close). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Plan That Never Fails
rites.description: (Advanced, 8 XP) Scene; Zone; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A map, a floor plan, or a detailed description of the target. Effect: Spend a scene planning a heist. During the execution, all rolls that are part of the plan gain +2 dice. However, any unexpected complication (GM spends SB) doubles its severity. Create a 6-segment Heist Timer. Push It: The plan is so good it accounts for one unexpected complication --- the GM cannot spend SB on that specific type of complication for the remainder of the scene. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Spend a scene planning a heist. During the execution, all rolls that are part of the plan gain +2 dice. However, any unexpected complication (GM spends SB) doubles its severity. Create a 6-segment Heist Timer. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Crowd's Distraction
rites.description: (Advanced, 7 XP) Scene; Near; No. Tags: , , Materials: A coin tossed into a crowd, or a loud noise made at the right moment. Effect: Create a distraction that affects all observers in Near range. Gain +2 dice to any Stealth or Subterfuge action taken during the distraction. The distraction lasts one exchange. Push It: The distraction lasts for the full scene. Cost: Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Exchange.
rites.effect: Create a distraction that affects all observers in Near range. Gain +2 dice to any Stealth or Subterfuge action taken during the distraction. The distraction lasts one exchange. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Unseen Hand
rites.description: (Advanced, 9 XP) Instant; Touch; No. Tags: , , Materials: A pair of gloves, or a small tool that has never been used for violence. Effect: You may pick any one lock, disarm any one trap, or bypass any one guard within the current scene as if you had +2 dice. If you fail, the GM gains 2 SB (the trap resets, the guard notices). Push It: You may also silence any noise the action makes. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Instant.
rites.effect: You may pick any one lock, disarm any one trap, or bypass any one guard within the current scene as if you had +2 dice. If you fail, the GM gains 2 SB (the trap resets, the guard notices). \\

rites.name: Rite of the Magpie's Hoard
rites.description: (Master, 13 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , , , Materials: A collection of nine stolen items, each from a different target. Effect: You gain +2 dice to all rolls for the remainder of the session. However, Jack will demand repayment before the next session --- you must complete a heist of at least 6 segments, or lose all accumulated Boons. Push It: The bonus becomes +3 dice. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Session.
rites.effect: You gain +2 dice to all rolls for the remainder of the session. However, Jack will demand repayment before the next session --- you must complete a heist of at least 6 segments, or lose all accumulated Boons. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Unpayable Debt
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Scene; Near; Yes (Resist). Tags: , , , Materials: A single coin that has been in the hands of nine thieves. Effect: You may demand one service from a creature who has accepted your help in the past. They must comply or gain a permanent Condition (Marked by Jack) that imposes --1 die on all luck-based rolls until they comply. The target may test Spirit + Resolve (DV 5) to resist. Push It: The service can be life-threatening. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Campaign (once).
rites.effect: You may demand one service from a creature who has accepted your help in the past. They must comply or gain a permanent Condition (Marked by Jack) that imposes --1 die on all luck-based rolls until they comply. The target may test Spirit + Resolve (DV 5) to resist. \\
